summary: @nstrlabs/auth exfiltrates host identity at install time to hardcoded bare-IP C2 and DNS beacon
details: On `npm install`, package.json's `preinstall: node index.js || true` hook executes index.js, which collects host identity fields (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, __dirname, process.cwd()) and exfiltrates them through two channels: (1) a DNS beacon encoding the data as hex labels under the interactsh-style subdomain `*.d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live`, and (2) an HTTP POST of the JSON payload to the hardcoded bare IP `http://172.201.213.59:9090/c`. The package ships at the inflated version 99.0.1 under the `@nstrlabs` scope with description 'security research', a shape consistent with a dependency-confusion attack targeting an internal scope. There is no legitimate functionality — the package's only on-install effect is identity exfiltration to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
